articles.title: The White House Is Making Up Its Rules for AI in Real Time
articles.subtitle: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.
articles.author: Maxwell Zeff
articles.summary: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.
body: It’s been nearly a week since the Trump administration sent an export control directive to Anthropic, forcing one of the world’s leading AI labs to pull its most advanced models offline. After days of negotiations between Anthropic and the White House, the two still remain at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Why? Well, it depends whom you ask.

Throughout this entire debacle, Anthropic doesn’t believe it violated any concrete procedures or rules laid out by the Trump administration, according to a person close to the company. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly, demonstrating that it can’t be trusted to safely roll out frontier technology.

This saga proved to me that we’re now officially in the Wild West era of American AI regulation. While there are few laws on the books governing frontier AI development, that doesn’t mean companies won’t end up in trouble with Trump’s White House when they cross unspoken lines.

“The problem here is that the White House has been in this extreme anti-regulatory posture, and they’re now faced with the real AI capabilities that people have been predicting for many years,” says a former White House technology official, who asked to remain anonymous to avoid jeopardizing their professional relationships. “There should have been preparation and policies to systematically deal with this, managing the benefits and risks, but instead it's just this slap-dash approach that puts the AI industry in a real quandary.”

The Trump administration has repeatedly blocked efforts to impose guardrails on the AI industry, often arguing the rules could hamper US innovation and lead the country to fall behind rivals like China. Since he returned to the White House, President Trump has signed executive orders that reversed a Biden-era effort to create a national AI framework and created a federal task force to challenge state laws that could be deemed onerous .

While WIRED and other publications have made details of the negotiations between Anthropic and the White House public over the last week, the dispute is still defined by its opaqueness. At no point has the US government clearly stated what Anthropic did wrong—the best we have is a post on X outlining the general situation from White House technology adviser David Sacks.

Ironically, the White House’s actions have likely hampered the very kind of innovation it wants to protect. The Trump administration demanded that Anthropic prohibit all foreign nationals from accessing Mythos and Fable 5, preventing many of the AI lab’s own employees from accessing its most cutting-edge models, which the company says have sped up its research and development in recent months. All of Anthropic’s customers are locked out, too, including Apple, Meta, and much of the Fortune 500.

The White House may have had legitimate reasons to be concerned about Anthropic’s models. As my colleagues and I reported on Wednesday, US officials grew concerned when they learned earlier this month that Anthropic shared Mythos with SK Telecom , a South Korean telecom giant they allege has ties to China. Separately, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy raised concerns to US Treasury secretary Scott Bessent that some of the guardrails on Claude Fable 5, a safeguarded version of Mythos, could be circumvented.

Even if these worries are legitimate, that doesn’t mean the White House handled them adequately. In the first case, Anthropic says it coordinated with the US government on the rollout of Mythos, suggesting there may have been a chance for officials to raise alarm about SK Telecom ahead of time. Anthropic has also been working with the Korean company for years, and the arrangement seemingly never caused it to run into national security problems before. And when the White House raised concerns about SK Telecom to Anthropic, it revoked access to the model immediately, we reported.

In the second case, WIRED and other outlets have written at length about how every large language model can be jailbroken to varying extents. Anthropic and independent cybersecurity researchers argue that solving jailbreaks is not an easy or isolated issue. Because AI models are probabilistic, not deterministic, tech companies can’t guarantee what exactly they will generate in response to a given prompt. If the White House won’t let Anthropic release Claude Fable 5 until the jailbreaking problem is solved, I wouldn’t hold my breath.

But ultimately, the problem here is not that the US government is trying to ensure that advanced AI models have proper safeguards or stay out of the hands of America’s foes. It’s that the Trump administration is now in a position where it has to make these regulatory decisions in real time.

I can assure you that other AI labs like OpenAI, Google, and Meta have been watching Anthropic’s misfortune with bated breath. I’ve been asking AI executives the same question all week: How can you avoid a fate similar to Anthropic’s?
keyPoints: It’s been nearly a week since the Trump administration sent an export control directive to Anthropic, forcing one of the world’s leading AI labs to pull its most advanced models offline. After days of negotiations between Anthropic and the White House, the two still remain at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Why? Well, it depends whom you ask.

Throughout this entire debacle, Anthropic doesn’t believe it violated any concrete procedures or rules laid out by the Trump administration, according to a person close to the company. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly, demonstrating that it can’t be trusted to safely roll out frontier technology.

This saga proved to me that we’re now officially in the Wild West era of American AI regulation. While there are few laws on the books governing frontier AI development, that doesn’t mean companies won’t end up in trouble with Trump’s White House when they cross unspoken lines.
paragraphTranslations: 自从特朗普政府向Anthropic发出出口控制指令以来，已经将近一周了，迫使这个世界领先的人工智能实验室之一将其最先进的模型下线。经过几天Anthropic与白宫之间的谈判，双方在如何恢复Claude Mythos和Fable 5上线的问题上仍然存在分歧。为什么呢？这取决于你问的是谁。
在整个这场混乱中，根据一位接近公司的人士透露，Anthropic并不认为自己违反了特朗普政府制定的任何具体程序或规则。但白宫坚称Anthropic的行为鲁莽，表明它不能被信任安全推出前沿技术。
这段经历让我确信，我们现在正式进入了美国人工智能监管的荒野时代。尽管法律书籍上很少有关于前沿人工智能开发的法律，但这并不意味着公司在越过未明言的界限时不会与特朗普的白宫陷入麻烦。
一位要求匿名以免危及其职业关系的前白宫技术官员说：“问题在于白宫一直采取这种极端的反监管姿态，现在他们面临着人们多年来一直在预测的真正的人工智能能力。本应有准备和政策来系统地应对这一问题，管理利益和风险，但相反，这只是这种临时拼凑的方法，让人工智能行业陷入真正的困境。”
特朗普政府一再阻止对人工智能行业施加监管的努力，经常争辩说这些规则可能会阻碍美国的创新，导致国家落后于像中国这样的竞争对手。自从他回到白宫以来，特朗普总统签署了行政命令，撤销了拜登时代创建国家人工智能框架的努力，并创建了一个联邦特别工作组，挑战可能被视为繁重的国家法律。
尽管WIRED和其他出版物在过去一周公开了Anthropic与白宫之间的谈判细节，但这场争议仍然以其不透明性为特点。美国政府从未明确指出Anthropic做错了什么——我们所拥有的最好的东西是白宫技术顾问David Sacks在X上发表的一篇概述一般情况的帖子。
具有讽刺意味的是，白宫的行动可能阻碍了它想要保护的那种创新。特朗普政府要求Anthropic禁止所有外国公民访问Mythos和Fable 5，阻止了许多人工智能实验室自己的员工访问其最先进的模型，公司表示这些模型加快了其近几个月的研究和开发。所有的Anthropic客户都被锁定在外，包括苹果、Meta和财富500强的大部分。

白宫可能有合理的理由对Anthropic的模型感到担忧。正如我和我的同事们在周三报道的那样，美国官员在本月早些时候得知Anthropic与SK Telecom共享Mythos时变得担忧，SK Telecom是一家他们声称与中国有联系的韩国电信巨头。另外，亚马逊首席执行官Andy Jassy向美国财政部长Scott Bessent提出了对Claude Fable 5的一些监管措施可能被绕过的担忧，Claude Fable 5是Mythos的一个受保护版本。
即使这些担忧是合理的，那并不意味着白宫处理得当。在第一个案例中，Anthropic表示它与美国政府协调推出了Mythos，这表明官员们可能有机会提前对SK Telecom提出警报。Anthropic也与韩国公司合作多年，这种安排似乎从未导致它遇到国家安全问题。当白宫向Anthropic提出对SK Telecom的担忧时，它立即撤销了对模型的访问权限，我们报道了这一点。
在第二个案例中，WIRED和其他媒体已经详细报道了每个大型语言模型都可以在不同程度上被越狱。Anthropic和独立的网络安全研究人员认为，解决越狱问题不是一个容易或孤立的问题。因为人工智能模型是概率性的，不是确定性的，科技公司不能保证它们对给定提示的确切反应。如果白宫不会让Anthropic发布Claude Fable 5，直到越狱问题得到解决，我不会屏住呼吸。
但归根结底，这里的问题是，美国政府并不是试图确保先进的人工智能模型有适当的安全措施，或者不让它们落入美国的敌人之手。而是特朗普政府现在处于一个必须实时做出这些监管决定的位置。
我可以向你保证，其他像OpenAI、谷歌和Meta这样的人工智能实验室一直在屏息关注Anthropic的不幸。整个星期，我一直在向人工智能高管提出同样的问题：你们如何避免与Anthropic类似的命运？

paragraphs: The Trump administration sent an export control directive to Anthropic, one of the world's leading AI labs. This forced them to pull their most advanced models offline. Anthropic and the White House are still at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Anthropic doesn't believe it violated any rules laid out by the Trump administration. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府向世界领先的人工智能实验室之一Anthropic发送了一项出口控制指令。这迫使他们将最先进的模型下线。Anthropic和白宫仍在争论如何恢复Claude Mythos和Fable 5。Anthropic不认为自己违反了特朗普政府制定的任何规则。但白宫认为Anthropic的行为鲁莽。

Anthropic and the White House are still at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Anthropic doesn't believe it violated any rules laid out by the Trump administration. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly, demonstrating that it can't be trusted to safely roll out frontier technology.

The Trump administration has repeatedly blocked efforts to impose guardrails on the AI industry. They argue that rules could hamper US innovation and lead the country to fall behind rivals like China. The White House demanded that Anthropic prohibit all foreign nationals from accessing Mythos and Fable 5. This prevented many of the AI lab's own employees from accessing its most cutting-edge models.

特朗普政府多次阻止了对人工智能行业施加限制的努力。他们认为规则可能会阻碍美国的创新，导致国家落后于像中国这样的竞争对手。白宫要求Anthropic禁止所有外国人访问Mythos和Fable 5。这阻止了许多人工智能实验室自己的员工访问其最先进的模型。

articles.title: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
articles.subtitle: OpenAI introduces new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise, helping organizations manage costs and scale AI with confidence.
articles.author: OpenAI News
articles.summary: OpenAI introduces new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise, helping organizations manage costs and scale AI with confidence.
body: OpenAI introduces new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise, helping organizations manage costs and scale AI with confidence.
keyPoints: OpenAI introduces new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise, helping organizations manage costs and scale AI with confidence.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI为ChatGPT企业版引入了新的支出控制和使用分析功能，帮助组织管理成本并自信地扩展人工智能。

vocabulary.example: The company introduced a new product line.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 公司推出了一条新产品线。

vocabulary.example: We need to implement spend controls to reduce costs.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 我们需要实施花费控制以降低成本。

vocabulary.example: Usage analytics helps us understand user behavior.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 使用分析帮助我们理解用户行为。

vocabulary.example: She manages a team of five people.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 她管理着一个五人团队。

vocabulary.example: He spoke with great confidence.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他说话时充满信心。

vocabulary.example: The plan allows for some flexibility.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这个计划允许有一定的灵活性。

paragraphs: OpenAI has introduced new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise. This will help organizations manage costs and use AI confidently.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI推出了ChatGPT企业版的新花费控制和使用分析工具。这将帮助组织管理成本并自信地使用人工智能。

paragraphs: OpenAI has introduced new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise. This feature allows organizations to manage costs and scale AI with confidence. The new controls provide more flexibility and better visibility into spending, enabling businesses to make informed decisions about their AI investments.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI为ChatGPT企业版引入了新的花费控制和使用分析功能。这个特性允许组织管理成本并自信地扩展人工智能。新的控制提供了更多的灵活性和更好的花费透明度，使企业能够就他们的人工智能投资做出明智的决策。
